content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – model for contract_nli_no_align_dev_7_nda_13
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_7_nda_13

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Imports
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
open std.strings
open std.collections

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Parties
type Party = 
    | Company(unit)          // the receiving party (the Company)
    | AHPL(unit)             // the disclosing party (ALEXANDRA HEALTH PTE LTD)

@ Clause 1.2(a) – Confidential Information
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    source: Party,                     // who supplied the information
    content: string,                   // free‑form description (for illustration)
    is_similar_to_other: bool          // true if the information is “similar” to
                                      // information that could be obtained from a third party
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the concrete objects that will be aligned across modules
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – Parties (public model objects)
entity receiving_party: Party          // must be Company in this contract
entity disclosing_party: Party         // must be AHPL in this contract

@ Clause 1.2(a) – Confidential Information
entity ci: ConfidentialInfo

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2(c) – the source of the information is the Discloser
func is_from_ahpl(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.source = AHPL()

@ Clause 1.2(c) – the Receiving Party is the Company
func is_company(p: Party) -> bool:
    p = Company()

@ Clause 1.2(b) – similarity test (abstract – treated as a Boolean flag)
func is_similar(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.is_similar_to_other

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Obligations (O), Prohibitions (F) and Permissions (P)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.1(a) – Use only for the Purpose and no other purpose
@ Clause 2.1(a) – purpose‑limited use
rule O use_only_for_purpose:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies (ci.is_similar_to_other = false) always

# 2.1(b) – No copying beyond what is reasonably necessary
@ Clause 2.1(b) – copying restriction
rule O no_unnecessary_copy:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies (ci.is_similar_to_other = false) always

# 2.1(c) – Disclosure only to authorised persons
@ Clause 2.1(c) – limited disclosure
rule O disclose_only_to_authorised:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies
        (is_company(receiving_party) and
         (ci.is_similar_to_other = false)) always

# 2.1(d) – Responsibility for sub‑clauses
@ Clause 2.1(d) – responsibility for employees, directors, etc.
rule O responsible_for_subordinates:
    is_company(receiving_party) always

# 2.1(e) – Security measures at least as strong as own
@ Clause 2.1(e) – security level
rule O security_not_less_than_own:
    is_company(receiving_party) always

# 2.2 – Liability for breach by agents
@ Clause 2.2 – principal liability
rule O principal_liability:
    is_company(receiving_party) always

# 2.3 – Permission to disclose required by law (with notice)
@ Clause 2.3 – lawful disclosure
rule P disclose_by_law:
    true always   // permission is granted, but the rule body will be refined
                  // by the “notice” rule below

# 2.3 – Notice requirement for lawful disclosure
@ Clause 2.3 – notice to Discloser
rule O notice_before_lawful_disclosure:
    true eventually   // the model must ensure a notice is given before any
                  // lawful disclosure; the exact timing is left to the solver

# 3 – Return of Confidential Information
@ Clause 3 – return on completion or request
rule O return_on_completion_or_request:
    true eventually   // the information must be returned within one month;
                       // the exact deadline is expressed by the temporal operator

# 4.1 – Data remains property of AHPL
@ Clause 4.1 – ownership
rule O data_ownership:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies (ci.source = AHPL()) always

# 4.2 – Reasonable precautions
@ Clause 4.2 – data protection
rule O reasonable_precautions:
    is_company(receiving_party) always

# 4.3 – Erasure on termination
@ Clause 4.3 – data erasure
rule O erase_on_termination:
    true eventually   // must be performed when the agreement ends

# 4.5 – No commercial use of data
@ Clause 4.5 – prohibition on commercial exploitation
rule F commercial_use_of_data:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies (ci.is_similar_to_other = false) always

# 5.1 – No licence granted
@ Clause 5.1 – licence prohibition
rule F licence_granted:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies false always   // cannot grant any licence

# 6 – Confidentiality of the Agreement itself
@ Clause 6 – keep agreement existence confidential
rule O keep_agreement_confidential:
    true always

# 7 – Injunctive relief (not modelled as a constraint, but noted)
@ Clause 7 – equitable relief (informational only)

# 8.3 – Recipient must not register IP on the Information
@ Clause 8.3 – IP registration prohibition
rule F register_ip_on_ci:
    is_from_ahpl(ci) implies false always

# 9 – Limitation of liability (not a direct constraint on entities)

# 10 – Notices (informational)

# 11 – Termination (survival of clauses model8,11‑15,17‑21)
@ Clause 11 – survival of key obligations
rule O survival_of_obligations:
    true always

# 12 – Non‑assignment (informational)

# 13 – Severability (informational)

# 14 – Waiver (informational)

# 15 – Dispute resolution (informational)

# 16 – Professional fees (informational)

# 17 – Export control (informational)

# 18 – Entire agreement (informational)

# 19 – No partnership (informational)

# 20 – Governing law (informational)

# 21 – Miscellaneous (informational)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis – “Receiving Party may acquire information similar to Confidential
# Information from a third party.”
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Hypothesis derived from the NDA text
rule P acquire_similar_info_from_third_party:
    is_company(receiving_party) and
    (ci.is_similar_to_other = true) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facts (optional – used for testing only; should be removed in final model)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# fact example_party = Company()
# fact example_disclosing = AHPL()
# fact example_ci = { source = AHPL(), content = "sample", is_similar_to_other = true }

# End of module
```
